input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for their .NET project. They haven't run coverage yet, so I need to:

1. Explore the project structure
2. Set up and run tests with code coverage
3. Analyze the results

Let me start by exploring the codebase structure.

data.content: Good — I can see the project structure. The test project already has `coverlet.collector` installed. Let me run tests with coverage collection.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-1c88f753e86d40bb967db6533a6fd221 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --verbosity quiet 2>&1

data.reasoningText: Now I have a good understanding of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage using coverlet (which is already a dependency), then analyze the results.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-1c88f753e86d40bb967db6533a6fd221 && dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --results-directory ./TestResults --verbosity quiet 2>&1

data.content: Tests passed. Now let me install `reportgenerator` and parse the coverage data.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-1c88f753e86d40bb967db6533a6fd221 && dotnet tool install -g dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool --verbosity quiet 2>&1 && export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/.dotnet/tools" && reportgenerator -reports:"./TestResults/*/coverage.cobertura.xml" -targetdir:./CoverageReport -reporttypes:"TextSummary" 2>&1 && cat ./CoverageReport/Summary.txt
